description: rsa::pss::Signature::try_from() is a newtype wrapper that accepts any byte slice length. The RsaPss2048Sha256 algorithm name advertises 2048-bit (256-byte) signatures, but a backend returning 128 bytes (1024-bit RSA) or 384 bytes (3072-bit RSA) is silently accepted by the adapter and returned to callers. A backend misconfiguration or bug that downgrades from RSA-2048 to RSA-1024 would pass undetected. Integrity risk: callers trust RsaPssSigner to enforce the 2048-bit key size contract, but the adapter does not. Fix: assert bytes.len() == 256 before calling try_from, returning signature::Error if the length is wrong.
title: RsaPssSigner: no key-size enforcement on raw bytes
